item.author: lalami

item.title: Terraforming AWS RDS：Postgres 实用指南 | Terraforming AWS RDS : A practical guide with Postgres
item.description:
卢卡斯·巴雷特

发表于 6 月 8 日

我最近写了一系列关于 PostgreSQL 的文章，并介绍了讲故事。 讲故事的写作很有趣，我会写更多这样的文章。

尽管如此，我还是想返璞归真，只从技术方面重写一些东西。 请不要犹豫，就您喜欢哪种文章向我提供任何反馈。 在本文中，我们将深入了解 AWS 基础知识，目标是最终得到一个完整的 terraform 文件，以部署一个公开可用的单个可用区 RDS 数据库。

创建可访问的网络

要创建可供公众访问的数据库，我们需要先创建一个网络。 这将需要几个组件，首先是一个 VPC，然后是一个 Internet 网关、子网，以及带有路由表的所有内容。

创建专有网络

首先，我们需要配置一个 VPC。 该虚拟专用网络具有一系列与其他网络隔离的网络地址。 目前，如果没有任何额外组件，这些地址无法在您的 VPC 外部访问。

一个 VPC 位于一个区域中，可以服务于该区域的每个可用区。 如果您想深入了解 VPC，可以访问 AWS文档

当您想要部署 VPC 并需要您的架构可审计、可测试和可重复时，您可以使用 Terraform。 这就是 Terraform 部署 VPC 的样子。

 resource "aws_vpc" "main" { cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16" instance_tenancy = "default" tags = { Name = "main" } }

使用此代码，您将部署一个地址范围为10.0.0.0到10.0.255.255 VPC，代表 65,536 个地址。

如果你想避免用头或手计算地址数量，有这个cidr calculator ，但这只是一个例子; 那里有很多！

我们把instance_tenancy = default ; 这使我们能够使用任何 RDS 数据库。 另一个可能的值是dedicated 。 但最好确保您的数据库被批准使用dedicated模式。

分而治之

如果我们想要创建 AWS 资源的任何实例，我们必须创建一个子网。 在我们的例子中，我们将在我们的 VPC 中创建两个子网。

 variable "subnets_cidr" { type = list(string) default = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24"] } variable "azs" { type = list(string) default = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"] } resource "aws_subnet" "public" { count = length(var.subnets_cidr) vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id cidr_block = element(var.subnets_cidr, count.index) availability_zone = element(var.azs, count.index) map_public_ip_on_launch = true tags = { Name = "Subnet-${count.index + 1}" } }

太好了，我们已经创建了两个子网。 这两个子网各有 255 个地址。 这些子网各自位于不同的可用性区域。 第一个在us-east-1a中，子范围为10.0.1.0/24 ，第二个在us-east-1b中，子范围为10.0.2.0/24 。 路由到子网

如果我们希望入口和出口流量能够很好地重定向，我们必须创建一个路由表并将该路由表与我们的子网相关联。

在 Terraform 中，这是特殊的； 您有可用于将路由表与子网相关联的资源。
但这不是 AWS 资源； 这是一个抽象。

您无法在 AWS 中的任何位置创建route table association对象。 实际上，当您直接创建route table时，您可以将其修改为它所连接的子网。

 resource "aws_route_table" "public-rt" { vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id route { cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0" gateway_id = aws_internet_gateway.gw.id } tags = { Name = "public-rt" } } resource "aws_route_table_association" "a" { count = length(var.subnets_cidr) subnet_id = element(aws_subnet.public.*.id, count.index) route_table_id = aws_route_table.public-rt.id }

是时候处理严肃的事情了

开始吧，是时候处理本文的核心了：数据库。

数据库子网组

要部署我们的数据库，我们需要两种资源。 第一个是您可以想象的aws_db_instance 。 但是我们需要一个aws_db_subnet_group 。

这些db subnet groups是什么？ 让我解释！

一个数据库子网组包含多个子网。 在构建我们的 db 子网组时，我们必须为子网 ID 至少提供两个子网，正如我们在上面看到的那样。

安全

安全应该是您最关心的问题之一； 控制进出交通是必要的。 特别是，在可公开访问的数据库的情况下

我们需要描述哪些流量可以进入，哪些不能进入。 这由 Terraform 中的aws_security_group管理。

在此资源中，您将描述允许访问的端口、该端口上的协议以及授权的 IP 地址。 您将为流出的流量执行相同的操作。

 resource "aws_security_group" "allow_postgres_traffic" { name = "allow_postgres" description = "Allow Postgres traffic" vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id ingress { description = "allow Postgres" from_port = 5432 to_port = 5432 protocol = "tcp" cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] } egress { from_port = 0 to_port = 0 protocol = "-1" cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] ipv6_cidr_blocks = ["::/0"] } tags = { Name = "allow postgres" } }

在那种情况下，没有这种保护。 确实，我们授权任何IP地址都可以访问5432端口。当然这个要根据自己的需要进行调整，限制比较多。

差不多了

现在我们必须部署我们的数据库。 这很简单，属性不言自明。 我们想在主 VPC 中部署一个 t2.micro。 这个数据库应该可以在之前部署的安全组中公开访问。

 resource "aws_db_instance" "db-test1" { allocated_storage = 10 identifier = "postgres-test2" db_subnet_group_name = aws_db_subnet_group.default.id engine = "postgres" engine_version = "14" instance_class = "db.t3.micro" username = "postgres" password = "postgres" vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group. allow_postgres_traffic.id] publicly_accessible = true skip_final_snapshot = true }

部署它

感谢您跟随我。 通过本文，我们了解了如何在 AWS 上部署可公开访问的数据库。

为此，我们需要部署各种资源：

  • 一个 VPC，以确保我们的资源有一个专用虚拟网络。
  • 用于划分我们的专用网络的子网； 如果我们现在或将来要部署多可用区架构，这是必需的。
  • 链接子网和数据库的数据库子网组
  • 路由表，用于向内路由外部流量和

如果您想测试我们在这里构建的内容，我创建了一个要点，其中提供了整个文件。

一旦暂停，yet_anotherdev 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，yet_anotherdev 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，yet_anotherdev 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 yet_anotherdev 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 yet_anotherdev：

降低 yet_anotherdev 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging yet_anotherdev will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagging yet_anotherdev 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Lucas Barret

Posted on Jun 8

 

I have recently written a series of articles about PostgreSQL and introduced storytelling. The writing with storytelling was fun, and I will write more articles like this.

Nevertheless, I want to return to the basics and rewrite something with only the technical aspects. Please do not hesitate to give me any feedback about what kind of article you prefer. :)

In this article, we will dive into AWS basics and aim to end up with an entire terraform file to deploy a single availability zone RDS database that is available publicly.

Create an accessible network

To create our database accessible to the public, we will need to create a network first. This will require several components, first a VPC, then an Internet Gateway, subnetworks, and everything sprinkled with a routing table.

Create a VPC

First thing first, we need to configure a VPC. VPC is an acronym for Virtual Private Cloud. This virtual private network has a range of network addresses isolated from other networks. These addresses are not accessible outside your VPC, for now, without any extra component.

A VPC is located in a region and can serve each availability zone of this region. If you want to dig more about VPC, you can go to the AWS documentation

You could use Terraform when you want to deploy a VPC and need your architecture to be auditable, testable, and repeatable. This is what a terraform could look like for deploying a VPC.

 resource "aws_vpc" "main" { cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16" instance_tenancy = "default" tags = { Name = "main" }
}

With this code, you will deploy a VPC with a range of addresses from 10.0.0.0 to 10.0.255.255, representing 65,536 addresses.

If you want to avoid computation of the number of addresses by head or hand, there is this cidr calculator, but this is only an example; plenty is out there!

We put the instance_tenancy = default; this enables us to work with any RDS database. The other possible value for this is dedicated. But it would be best to ensure your database is approved to work with the dedicated mode.

Divide ut regnes

If we want to create any instances of AWS resources, we must create a subnetwork. In our case, we will create two subnetworks in our VPC.

variable "subnets_cidr" { type = list(string) default = ["10.0.1.0/24", "10.0.2.0/24"]
} variable "azs" { type = list(string) default = ["us-east-1a", "us-east-1b"]
} resource "aws_subnet" "public" { count = length(var.subnets_cidr) vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id cidr_block = element(var.subnets_cidr, count.index) availability_zone = element(var.azs, count.index) map_public_ip_on_launch = true tags = { Name = "Subnet-${count.index + 1}" }
}

Excellent, we have created two subnetworks. These two subnetworks have 255 addresses each. These subnets are each in a different availability zone. The first one is in us-east-1a and has the subrange 10.0.1.0/24, and the second one is in us-east-1b and has the subrange 10.0.2.0/24. (If you want to train, put in a comment: What is the number of addresses available in these two subnetworks)

The element(var.name) iterates over the value of a list and assign one value, but we need the count = length(var.subnets_cidr) to declare a loop.

Routing towards the subnet

If we want the ingress and egress traffic to be well redirected, we must create a route table and associate this route table with our subnetworks.

In Terraform, this is special; you have a resource that is available to associate your route table with your subnetworks.
But this is not an AWS resource; this is an abstraction.

You cannot create a route table association object anywhere in AWS. Indeed when you create your route table directly, you can modify it to what subnetworks it is attached to.

resource "aws_route_table" "public-rt" { vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id route { cidr_block = "0.0.0.0/0" gateway_id = aws_internet_gateway.gw.id } tags = { Name = "public-rt" }
} resource "aws_route_table_association" "a" { count = length(var.subnets_cidr) subnet_id = element(aws_subnet.public.*.id, count.index) route_table_id = aws_route_table.public-rt.id
}

Time to deal with serious things

Here we go, time to deal with the heart of this article: the database.

DB subnet groups

To deploy our database, we need two resources. The first one is the aws_db_instance as you can imagine. But we need an aws_db_subnet_group.

What are these db subnet groups? Let me explain!

A DB subnet group contains several subnets. AWS RDS chooses a subnet from the DB subnet group, gives the database an IP address, and affects it to an availability zone.

resource "aws_db_subnet_group" "default" { name = "main" subnet_ids = [aws_subnet.public[0].id, aws_subnet.public[1].id] tags = { Name = "My DB subnet group" }
}

When building our db subnet group, we must give it at least two subnets for the subnets ids, as we have seen above.

Security

Security should be one of your top concerns; controlling traffic in and out is necessary. In particular, in the case of publicly accessible database

We need to describe what traffic can go in and what cannot. This is managed by the aws_security_group in Terraform.

In this resource, you will describe the port you allow access to, the protocol on that port, and the IP address entitled. You will do the same for the traffic that goes out.

resource "aws_security_group" "allow_postgres_traffic" { name = "allow_postgres" description = "Allow Postgres traffic" vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id ingress { description = "allow Postgres" from_port = 5432 to_port = 5432 protocol = "tcp" cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] } egress { from_port = 0 to_port = 0 protocol = "-1" cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] ipv6_cidr_blocks = ["::/0"] } tags = { Name = "allow postgres" }
}

In that case, there is no such protection. Indeed, we authorize any IP address to access port 5432. Of course, this has to be tuned to your needs, with more restrictions.

Almost there

Now we have to deploy our database. This is straightforward, and the attributes speak for themselves. We want to deploy a t2.micro in the main VPC. This database should be publicly accessible within the security group deployed before.

resource "aws_db_instance" "db-test1" { allocated_storage = 10 identifier = "postgres-test2" db_subnet_group_name = aws_db_subnet_group.default.id engine = "postgres" engine_version = "14" instance_class = "db.t3.micro" username = "postgres" password = "postgres" vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group. allow_postgres_traffic.id] publicly_accessible = true skip_final_snapshot = true
}

Deploy it

Thanks for following along with me. Through this article, we have seen how to deploy a publicly accessible database on AWS.

To do so, we need to deploy a variety of resources:

  • A VPC to ensure we have a Private virtual network for our resources.
  • Subnets to divide our Private Network; this is needed if we want to deploy a multi-AZ architecture, now or in the future.
  • A DB subnet group That links the subnet and a database
  • A routing table that will route the external traffic inward and

I have created a gist where the whole file is available if you want to test what we have built here.

Once suspended, yet_anotherdev will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, yet_anotherdev will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by yet_anotherdev will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If yet_anotherdev is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag yet_anotherdev:

Make all posts by yet_anotherdev less visible

yet_anotherdev consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging yet_anotherdev will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: yet_anotherdev

item.title: ASP.NET Core 动作过滤器中的依赖注入 | Dependency injection in action filters in ASP.NET Core
item.description:
使用过滤器属性来装饰 ASP.NET MVC 控制器操作以区分横切关注点和操作的主要关注点是很常见的。 有时这些过滤器需要使用其他组件，但属性的效率非常有限，并且不可能直接将依赖注入到属性中。

这篇文章着眼于 ASP.NET Core 中动作过滤器中注入依赖的一些不同技术。 我们讨论了在退后一步之前何时应该使用每种方法，以及何时可以单独处理问题以获得更清晰的解决方案。

您可以将静态值传递给构造函数和/或直接设置公共属性。

因为属性参数是在编译时求值的，所以它们应该是编译时常量。 因此，从 IoC 容器注入依赖项不是一种选择。 在我们希望属性可以访问另一个组件的情况下，我们必须使用变通方法。

让我们看看一些选项：

如果我们不能将组件注入到我们的属性中，那么下一个最佳选择似乎是从我们的 IoC 容器中请求组件（直接或通过包装器）。

在 .NET 核心中，我们可以使用服务位置从使用 RequestServices.GetService 的内置 IoC 容器中解析组件：

 public class ThrottleFilterAttribute : Attribute, IActionFilter { public void OnActionExecuting(ActionExecutingContext context) { var cache = context.HttpContext.RequestServices.GetService<idistributedcache>(); ... } ... } </idistributedcache>

请注意，为了使用 GetService 的通用版本，您需要添加以下语句：

 using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;

这会起作用，但不推荐。 与构造函数注入不同，使用服务定位器（反）模式来解决您的依赖性要困难得多。 构造函数注入的美妙之处在于简单。 只看构造函数的定义，我们马上就知道该类所依赖的所有类。 服务位置使单元测试更加困难，这需要您对被测对象的内部结构有更多的了解。

阅读更多： Asp.net Core 中的哈希、加密和随机

服务过滤器属性

用法非常简单：

 [ServiceFilter(typeof(ThrottleFilter))]

这意味着我们可以更改我们的操作过滤器以直接从构造函数接受依赖项：

 public class ThrottleFilter :IActionFilter { private readonlyIDistributedCache _cache; public ThrottleFilter(IDistributedCache cache) { _cache = cache ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(cache)); } ... }

自然地，当我们从 IoC 容器解析我们的过滤器时，我们需要注册它：

 public void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) { ... services.AddScoped<throttlefilter>(); ... } </throttlefilter>

如果我们修改我们的 ThrottleFilter 示例以添加配置属性（同时保留 IDistributedCache 依赖项），那么 servicefilter 将不再对我们有用。 然而，我们可以使用 typefilter。

 public class ThrottleFilter :IActionFilter { private readonlyIDistributedCache _cache; public intMaxRequestPerSecond{ get; set; } public ThrottleFilter(IDistributedCache cache, intmaxRequestPerSecond) { _cache = cache ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(cache)); MaxRequestPerSecond = maxRequestPerSecond; } }

正在解析的类型不需要在 IoC 容器中注册。

  • 可以提供在构造过滤器时使用的参数

    • 全局动作过滤器

    到目前为止，我们只讨论了作为属性实现的过滤器，但您也可以全局应用过滤器：

     public void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) { services.AddMvc(options => { options.Filters.Add<throttlefilter>(); }); } </throttlefilter>

    这允许我们以与在操作或控制器上使用 servicefilter 属性相同的方式从 IoC 容器解析过滤器，但相反，它将应用于每个控制器的每个操作。 即使您需要能够配置单个操作，也不能使用全局过滤器隔离。

    被动属性

    如果我们考虑一下，动作过滤器属性容纳复杂的逻辑和与其他组件的交互是没有意义的。 属性最适合识别和选择我们属于哪个动作或控制器。 它们也最适合控制器的单独操作或配置。 他们可能不应该充满复杂的代码。

    操作上存在 ThrottleAttribute 表示它应该被限制。 这是一种选择加入的方法，但我们可以轻松地使用选择退出的方法，其中一个属性表明我们不想限制操作。

    正在寻找可靠的.NET 开发公司？ 今天咨询。

    我们还希望能够交替参考每秒最大请求数。 实现很简单：

     public class ThrottleAttribute : Attribute, IFilterMetadata { public intMaxRequestPerSecond{ get; set; } }

    请注意，我们应用了 IFilterMetadata。 这是一个标记界面，可以更轻松地从全局过滤器中读取我们的属性。

    全局操作过滤器以与本节开头所示相同的方式向 MVC 注册。 所有依赖项都由内置的 IoC 容器自动解析。

     public class ThrottleFilter :IActionFilter { private readonlyIDistributedCache _cache; public ThrottleFilter(IDistributedCache cache) { _cache = cache ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(cache)); } public void OnActionExecuted(ActionExecutedContext context) { //noop } public void OnActionExecuting(ActionExecutingContext context) { varthrottleAttribute = context.ActionDescriptor.FilterDescriptors .Select(x =>x.Filter).OfType<throttleattribute>().FirstOrDefault(); if (throttleAttribute != null) { } } } </throttleattribute>

    结论

    不可能将组件直接注入到动作过滤器属性中，但有多种变通方法可以有效地完成同样的事情。 使用 servicefilter 是一种相对干净的方法，可以将依赖项注入到单个操作过滤器中。 然而，以这种方式指定过滤器的类型确实意味着可以输入无效类型并且直到运行时才会发现。

    在实施任何这些技术之前，您应该考虑被动属性方法。 在我们看来，这是解决问题的最佳方案。 总而言之，此方法需要两个类。 我们将一个非常简单的标记属性应用于操作，并将其与具有真实逻辑的全局操作过滤器相结合。



    原文:

    It is quite common to decorate the ASP.NET MVC controller actions with filter attributes to differentiate cross-cutting concerns from the main concern of the action. Sometimes these filters require the use of other components but the attributes are very limited in their efficiency and dependence injection into an attribute is not directly possible.

    This post looks at some of the different techniques for injection dependence in action filters in the ASP.NET Core. We discuss when each method should be used before taking a step back and when we can approach the problem separately for a cleaner solution.

    DI and action filter attributes

    The Attributes in C # are very simple. You can pass static values to the constructor and/or set public properties directly.

    Because attribute parameters are evaluated at compile time, they should be compiled time constant. So injecting dependencies from an IoC container is not an option. In a situation where we want an attribute to have access to another component, we must use a workaround.

    Let's look at some options:

    The RequestServices.GetService Service Locator

    If we cannot inject a component into our attribute, it seems that the next best option is to request the component from our IoC container (either directly or by wrapper).

    In the .NET core, we can use the service location to resolve components from a built-in IoC container using the RequestServices.GetService:

        public class ThrottleFilterAttribute : Attribute, IActionFilter
{
    public void OnActionExecuting(ActionExecutingContext context)
    {
        var cache = context.HttpContext.RequestServices.GetService<idistributedcache>();
        ...
    }
    ...
}
</idistributedcache>

    Note that in order to use the generic version of the GetService you need to add the following statements:

    using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;

    This will work but is not recommended. Unlike constructor injection, it can be much harder to work out your dependence with the service locator (anti)pattern. The beauty of constructor injection is simplicity. Just by looking at the definition of a constructor, we immediately know all the classes on which the class depends. The service location makes the unit test harder which requires you to have more knowledge about the internals of the subject under test.

    Read More: Hashing, Encryption And Random In Asp.net Core

    The ServiceFilter attribute

    The servicefilter attribute may be used at the action or controller level. Usage is very straightforward:

    [ServiceFilter(typeof(ThrottleFilter))]

    The servicefilter attribute allows us to specify the type of our action filter and can automatically resolve the class from the built-in IoC container. This means we can change our action filter to accept dependencies directly from the constructor:

    public class ThrottleFilter :IActionFilter
{
    private readonlyIDistributedCache _cache;
    public ThrottleFilter(IDistributedCache cache)
    {
        _cache = cache ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(cache));
    }
    ...
}

    Naturally, as we are resolving our filters from the IoC container, we need to register it:

    public void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
{
    ...
services.AddScoped<throttlefilter>();
    ...
}
</throttlefilter>

    The TypeFilter attribute

    The servicefilter is very useful for attributes that have dependencies that need to be resolved from the IoC container but the lack of property support is a major limitation. If we modify our ThrottleFilter example to add configuration properties (while retaining IDistributedCache dependencies), then the servicefilter is no longer useful to us. We can however use typefilter.

    public class ThrottleFilter :IActionFilter
{
    private readonlyIDistributedCache _cache;
    public intMaxRequestPerSecond{ get; set; }
    public ThrottleFilter(IDistributedCache cache, intmaxRequestPerSecond)
    {
        _cache = cache ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(cache));
MaxRequestPerSecond = maxRequestPerSecond;
    }
}

    The typefilter is similar to the servicefilter, but there are two notable differences:

    • The type being resolved doesn't need to be registered with the IoC container..
    • Arguments can be provided that are used while constructing the filter

    Global action filters

    So far, we've only talked about filters implemented as attributes but you can also apply filters globally:

     public void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)
{
services.AddMvc(options =>
    {
options.Filters.Add<throttlefilter>();
    });
}
</throttlefilter>

    This allows us to resolve the filter from the IoC container in the same way as using a servicefilter attribute on an action or controller but instead, it will be applied to every action of each controller. You cannot use global filter isolation even if you need to be able to configure individual actions.

    Passive Attributes

    If we think about it, it does not make sense for action filter attributes to house complex logic and interactions with other components. Attributes are best for identification and by choosing which actions or controllers we belong to. They are also best for individual actions or configurations of controllers. They probably shouldn't be full of complex code.

    The presence of a ThrottleAttribute on the action indicates that it should be throttled. This is an opt-in approach but we can easily use an opt-out approach where an attribute indicates that we do not want to throttle the action.

    Searching for Reliable .NET Development Company? Enquire Today.

    We also want to be able to alternately refer to maximum requests per second. Implementation is trivial:

    public class ThrottleAttribute : Attribute, IFilterMetadata
{
    public intMaxRequestPerSecond{ get; set; }
}

    Note that we apply IFilterMetadata. This is a marker interface that makes it easier to read our attributes from the global filters.

    The global action filter is registered with MVC in the same way as we showed at the beginning of the section. All dependencies are resolved automatically by the built-in IoC container.

    public class ThrottleFilter :IActionFilter
{
    private readonlyIDistributedCache _cache;
    public ThrottleFilter(IDistributedCache cache)
    {
        _cache = cache ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(cache));
    }
    public void OnActionExecuted(ActionExecutedContext context)
    {
        //noop
    }
    public void OnActionExecuting(ActionExecutingContext context)
    {
varthrottleAttribute = context.ActionDescriptor.FilterDescriptors
.Select(x =>x.Filter).OfType<throttleattribute>().FirstOrDefault();
        if (throttleAttribute != null)
        {
        }
    }
}
</throttleattribute>

    Conclusion

    It is not possible to inject components directly into action filter attributes but there are various workarounds to allow the same thing to be accomplished effectively. Using a servicefilter is a relatively clean way to allow dependency injection into individual action filters. Specifying the type for the filter in this way does mean that invalid types can be entered and will not be discovered until runtime, however.

    Before implementing any of these techniques, you should consider a passive attribute approach. In our opinion, this is the best solution to the problem. To summarize, this method requires two classes. We have a very simple marker attribute applied to actions and this is combined with a global action filter that has real logic.
item.author: ifourtechnolab

item.title: 增强 B2B 电子商务：个性化和客户体验的力量 | Enhancing B2B Ecommerce: The Power of Personalization and Customer Experience
item.description:
随着世界变得越来越数字化，B2B 电子商务已成为商业成功的重要组成部分。 然而，随着市场竞争的不断加剧，企业必须想方设法从竞争对手中脱颖而出。 实现这一目标的一种方法是通过个性化和客户体验。

研究表明，客户体验是 B2B 电子商务成功的关键驱动因素。 事实上， 86% 的 B2B 买家愿意为出色的客户体验支付更多费用。 此外，客户更有可能对提供积极体验的企业保持忠诚。

转向个性化在线购物体验

近年来，电子商务中的个性化变得越来越流行，这是有充分理由的。 客户希望感受到被重视和赞赏，而个性化可以让企业做到这一点。 事实上，80% 的客户更有可能从提供个性化体验的企业购买商品。

了解 B2B 电子商务中的个性化

这可以包括产品推荐、内容和定价。

为 B2B 客户和企业提供个性化体验的好处包括提高客户满意度和忠诚度、提高转化率以及增加收入。 利用数据分析实现个性化

通过有效地捕获和利用客户数据，企业可以识别客户的偏好和行为，并使用这些信息来定制在线购物体验。

客户细分和定位在个性化中也很重要。 通过根据客户的行为和偏好将客户划分为特定的部分，企业可以提供更相关和个性化的体验。

人工智能和机器学习：实现个性化体验

预测分析和推荐引擎可用于提供个性化的产品推荐和定价。

案例研究表明，利用 AI 驱动的个性化的企业收入和客户参与度都有所增加。

在 B2B 电子商务平台中实施个性化

为实施有效的个性化策略，企业应根据客户行为定制产品推荐，利用动态定价策略，并针对特定客户群定制消息传递。

衡量和改进个性化成功

衡量个性化有效性的关键绩效指标 (KPI) 包括客户满意度和忠诚度指标。 收集客户的反馈和见解对于持续改进也至关重要。

超个性化等新兴趋势和技术将继续塑造 B2B 电子商务个性化的未来。 但是，企业还必须在个性化与数据隐私和安全考虑之间取得平衡。

Personalization and customer experience are crucial components of B2B ecommerce success. By leveraging data analytics, AI, and machine learning, businesses can provide personalized experiences that increase customer satisfaction, loyalty, and revenue. As customer expectations continue to evolve, businesses must embrace personalization to stay ahead of the curve.



原文:

As the world becomes increasingly digital, B2B ecommerce has become a crucial component of business success. However, as competition in the market continues to grow, businesses must find ways to differentiate themselves from their competitors. One way to achieve this is through personalization and customer experience.

Importance of customer experience in B2B ecommerce

Studies show that customer experience is a key driver of B2B ecommerce success. In fact, 86% of B2B buyers are willing to pay more for a great customer experience. Additionally, customers are more likely to remain loyal to a business that provides a positive experience.

Shift towards personalized online shopping experiences

Personalization in ecommerce has become increasingly popular in recent years, and for good reason. Customers want to feel valued and appreciated, and personalization allows businesses to do just that. In fact, 80% of customers are more likely to make a purchase from a business that provides personalized experiences.

Understanding Personalization in B2B Ecommerce

Personalization in B2B ecommerce refers to the process of tailoring the online shopping experience to meet the unique needs and preferences of each customer. This can include product recommendations, content, and pricing.

Benefits of personalized experiences for B2B customers and businesses include increased customer satisfaction and loyalty, higher conversion rates, and increased revenue. Personalization in B2B ecommerce differs from B2C ecommerce in that it often involves larger purchases and longer sales cycles.

Leveraging Data Analytics for Personalization

Data collection and analysis are crucial components of successful personalization in B2B ecommerce. By capturing and utilizing customer data effectively, businesses can identify customer preferences and behavior, and use this information to tailor the online shopping experience.

Customer segmentation and targeting are also important in personalization. By dividing customers into specific segments based on their behavior and preferences, businesses can provide more relevant and personalized experiences.

AI and Machine Learning:Enabling Personalized Experiences

AI and machine learning algorithms are increasingly being used to enhance personalization in B2B ecommerce. Predictive analytics and recommendation engines can be used to provide personalized product recommendations and pricing.

Case studies have shown that businesses that utilize AI-driven personalization see increased revenue and customer engagement.

Implementing Personalization in B2B Ecommerce Platforms

To implement effective personalization strategies, businesses should customize product recommendations based on customer behavior, utilize dynamic pricing strategies, and tailor messaging to specific customer segments.

Measuring and Improving Personalization Success

Key performance indicators (KPIs) for measuring personalization effectiveness include customer satisfaction and loyalty metrics. Gathering feedback and insights from customers is also crucial for continuous improvement.

The Future of Personalization in B2B Ecommerce

Emerging trends and technologies, such as hyper-personalization, will continue to shape the future of personalization in B2B ecommerce. However, businesses must also balance personalization with data privacy and security considerations.

Personalization and customer experience are crucial components of B2B ecommerce success. By leveraging data analytics, AI, and machine learning, businesses can provide personalized experiences that increase customer satisfaction, loyalty, and revenue. As customer expectations continue to evolve, businesses must embrace personalization to stay ahead of the curve.
item.author: keyurpate1

item.title: 每个前端开发人员都需要的 10 个免费图像工具 | 10 Free Image Tools Every Frontend Developer Needs
item.description:
介绍：

作为一名前端开发人员，使用图像是创建具有视觉吸引力和吸引力的 Web 项目不可或缺的一部分。 然而，优化和处理图像有时是一项艰巨的任务。 值得庆幸的是，有许多免费的图像工具可以简化您的工作流程、增强您的设计并优化图像以获得最佳的 Web 性能。 在本文中，我们将探讨每个前端开发人员的工具包中都应该拥有的十个基本免费图像工具。

小图片：

图像优化对于 Web 性能至关重要，而 TinyImage 是一款出色的工具，可以在不影响质量的情况下减小文件大小。 它专门用于压缩 PNG 和 JPEG 图像，可显着节省带宽并加快网页加载时间。

画布：

使用 Canva，你可以轻松地为你的网络项目创建令人惊叹的图像和图形，无论是设计横幅、社交媒体图形还是自定义插图。

它提供了一组广泛的工具和功能，使您能够精确地润饰照片、创建图形和处理图像。 不飞溅：

它提供了大量令人惊叹的照片库，您可以在您的 Web 项目中自由使用这些照片。 从风景到肖像，Unsplash 提供各种类别的各种图像。

像素：

使用 Pixlr，您可以执行基本的编辑任务、应用滤镜和效果、使用图层以及增强您的图像，而无需安装复杂的软件。

可缩放矢量图形(SVG) 对于创建可缩放且与分辨率无关的图像至关重要。 它确保您的 SVG 图像针对快速加载和平滑渲染进行了优化。

小JPG：

与TinyPNG类似，TinyJPG 专注于压缩 JPEG 图片。 它利用先进的压缩算法来减小文件大小，同时保持视觉质量。 通过使用 TinyJPG，您可以在不影响整体外观的情况下显着减小图像文件的大小。

图像优化：

它支持各种图像格式并采用不同的优化技术来确保您的图像尽可能小，同时保持其视觉完整性。

占位符：

在开发阶段，使用占位符图像是一种常见的做法。 它是一种方便的工具，可将视觉元素快速添加到您的布局中。

图玛：

虽然Figma主要被称为设计和原型制作工具，但它还提供强大的图像编辑功能。 它允许前端开发人员操作图像、调整颜色、裁剪和导出资产。 使用 Figma，您可以直接在设计工作流程中高效地处理与图像相关的任务。

结论：

在前端开发领域，优化和处理图像是创造卓越网络体验的一个重要方面。 本文提到的十种免费图像工具，包括 Canva、GIMP、TinyPNG、Unsplash、Pixlr、SVGOMG、TinyJPG、ImageOptim、Placeholder.com 和 Figma，可以显着改善您的图像处理工作流程。 通过使用这些工具，您可以增强您的设计，优化图像以提高网络性能，并节省图像编辑过程的时间。



原文:

Introduction:

As a frontend developer, working with images is an integral part of creating visually appealing and engaging web projects. However, optimizing and manipulating images can sometimes be a daunting task. Thankfully, there are numerous free image tools available that can streamline your workflow, enhance your designs, and optimize images for optimal web performance. In this article, we will explore ten essential free image tools that every frontend developer should have in their toolkit.

TinyImage:

TinyImage is the best image compression tool developed by the Attrock. Image optimization is crucial for web performance, and TinyImage is an excellent tool for reducing file sizes without compromising quality. It specializes in compressing PNG and JPEG images, providing significant savings in bandwidth and faster load times for your web pages.

Canva:

Canva is a versatile graphic design tool that provides a wide range of templates, elements, and editing features. With Canva, you can easily create stunning images and graphics for your web projects, whether it's designing banners, social media graphics, or custom illustrations.

GIMP:

GIMP (GNU Image Manipulation Program) is a powerful open-source image editing software. It offers an extensive set of tools and features, allowing you to retouch photos, create graphics, and manipulate images with precision. GIMP is a great alternative to paid software like Photoshop.

Unsplash:

Unsplash Finding high-quality, royalty-free images can be a challenge, but Unsplash makes it effortless. It offers a vast library of stunning photographs that you can use freely in your web projects. From landscapes to portraits, Unsplash provides a diverse range of images across various categories.

Pixlr:

Pixlr is a web-based image editing tool that offers a user-friendly interface resembling Photoshop. With Pixlr, you can perform basic editing tasks, apply filters and effects, work with layers, and enhance your images without the need for complex software installations.

SVGOMG:

Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) are essential for creating scalable and resolution-independent images. SVGOMG is an online tool that optimizes SVG files by reducing file sizes and cleaning up unnecessary code. It ensures that your SVG images are optimized for fast loading and smooth rendering.

TinyJPG:

Similar to TinyPNG, TinyJPG focuses on compressing JPEG images. It utilizes advanced compression algorithms to reduce file sizes while preserving visual quality. By using TinyJPG, you can significantly decrease image file sizes without compromising the overall appearance.

ImageOptim:

ImageOptim is a desktop application for macOS that optimizes images to reduce file sizes without sacrificing quality. It supports various image formats and employs different optimization techniques to ensure your images are as small as possible while maintaining their visual integrity.

Placeholder:

During the development phase, using placeholder images is a common practice. Placeholder generates custom placeholder images of any size, allowing you to create temporary image placeholders that fit your design requirements. It's a convenient tool for quickly adding visual elements to your layouts.

Figma:

While primarily known as a design and prototyping tool, Figma also offers powerful image editing capabilities. It allows frontend developers to manipulate images, adjust colors, crop, and export assets. With Figma, you can efficiently work on image-related tasks directly within your design workflow.

Conclusion:

In the world of frontend development, optimizing and manipulating images is a crucial aspect of creating exceptional web experiences. The ten free image tools mentioned in this article, including Canva, GIMP, TinyPNG, Unsplash, Pixlr, SVGOMG, TinyJPG, ImageOptim, Placeholder.com, and Figma, can significantly improve your image handling workflow. By utilizing these tools, you can enhance your designs, optimize images for web performance, and save time in the image editing process.
item.author: joywinter90

item.title: 如何使用截断 SVD 绘制特征重要性 | How to plot feature importance using Truncated SVD
item.description:
如何使用截断 SVD 从大量特征中选择和绘制最重要的特征！？

问题是什么？

正如我在之前的帖子中解释的那样，在MelkRadar的一个项目中，我不得不处理一个包含 1500 多个特征的大型数据集！ 识别最重要的特征确实让人不知所措，尤其是在特征转换之后。

这个问题是我通过特征选择和特征提取技术的旅程的开始。 我发现了两种著名的技术：截断 SVD 和 PCA。 毕竟，我知道截断 SVD 是处理我们的大型稀疏数据集问题的更好解决方案。

为什么在我的问题中截断 SVD 比 PCA 提供更多信息？

截断的 SVD 和 PCA：

截断 SVD（奇异值分解）和 PCA（主成分分析）都是线性代数技术。 它们是降维技术，用于降低高维数据集的维数。 它们都旨在找到数据的低维表示，同时指示最重要的信息。

截断 SVD 与 PCA：

  1. 截断 SVD 和 PCA 之间的主要区别在于它们处理数据的方式。 截断 SVD 通常首选用于文本数据，而 PCA 通常用于数值数据。

  2. 对于大型数据集，截断 SVD 通常比 PCA 更快，因为它只计算奇异向量和值的一个子集。

截断 SVD 如何识别最重要的特征？

截断 SVD not directly识别数据集中最频繁的特征。 其主要目标是在保留最重要信息的同时降低数据的维度。 但是，可以通过examining the singular vectors来间接识别最频繁的特征。

在截断 SVD 中，奇异向量是解释数据中最大方差的原始特征的线性组合。 因此，在奇异向量中具有最高系数的特征可以被认为是数据集中最重要的特征。

现在让我们做一些编码......

要使用截断 SVD 识别最频繁的特征，可以执行以下步骤：

步骤1：

为了简单起见，我生成了一个随机数据矩阵来模拟我在真实项目中拥有的数据集。

 from sklearn.decomposition import TruncatedSVD import numpy as np # Generate a random data matrix X of size (mxn) m, n = 1000, 1500 X = np.random.randn(m, n)

第2步：

现在将 X 拟合到截断的 SVD 以获得奇异向量。 然后，计算低秩近似值。

 k = 10 # number of singular vectors to keep U, S, Vt = np.linalg.svd(X) X_approx = U[:, :k] @ np.diag(S[:k]) @ Vt[:k, :] # fit data to the model

它是 am*m 矩阵。 该矩阵的列是 X 矩阵中奇异值的对应特征向量。

它是 am*n 矩阵。 它是 n*n 矩阵。 该矩阵的列是 X 矩阵中奇异值的相应特征向量。

请注意， U捕获 X 的relationships among the columns ，而Vt捕获 X 的relationships among the rows

第 3 步：

为了评估模型，我们必须像这样计算相对近似误差：

 approx_error = np.linalg.norm(X - X_approx) / np.linalg.norm(X) print(f'Relative approximation error: {approx_error:.2f}')

步骤4：

选择k个最重要的特征。 对于每个奇异向量，确定具有最高系数的特征。

第 5 步：

将特征重要性分数计算为前 k 个奇异向量中系数的绝对值之和。 计算所有奇异向量中每个特征的频率。

 feature_importance = np.abs(Vk).sum(axis=0)

第 6 步：

按降序对特征重要性分数进行排序。 按频率对特征进行排名，以识别数据集中最常见的特征。

 sorted_idx = np.argsort(feature_importance)[::-1] # Save the names of the top 10 most frequent features in a list top_features = [feature_names[i] for i in sorted_idx[:10]]

第 7 步：

创建前 10 个最常见特征的条形图：

 plt.barh(range(10), feature_importance[sorted_idx[:10]]) plt.yticks(range(10), top_features) plt.xlabel('Importance Score') plt.title('Most Frequent Features in Truncated SVD') plt.show()

注意...

这种方法假设最频繁的特征也是数据集中最重要的特征。 情况可能并非总是如此，因为如果重要特征与其他更频繁的特征相关，则它们可能具有较低的频率。

此外，截断 SVD 中截断参数的选择会影响结果，因此根据手头的问题选择合适的截断级别很重要。

一旦暂停，elldora 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，elldora 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，elldora 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 elldora 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 elldora 的方法：

降低 elldora 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging elldora will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning elldora 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

How to choose and plot the most important features from an overwhelming pool of features, using Truncated SVD!?

What was the problem?

As I explained in my previous post, in one of our projects at MelkRadar, I had to tackle with a large dataset including more than 1500 features! It was really overwhelming identify the most important features especially after the features transformation.

This problem was the beginning of my journey through the feature selection and feature extraction techniques. I found out about two well-known techniques: Truncated SVD and PCA. After all, I understood that Truncated SVD was a better solution to handle our problem with a large sparse dataset.

Why is the Truncated SVD more informative than PCA in my problem?

Truncated SVD and PCA:

Truncated SVD (Singular Value Decomposition) and PCA (Principal Component Analysis) are both linear algebra technique. They are dimensionality reduction techniques used to reduce the dimensionality of high-dimensional datasets. They both aim to find a lower-dimensional representation of the data while indicating the most important information.

Truncated SVD vs. PCA:

  1. The main difference between truncated SVD and PCA lies in how they handle data. Truncated SVD is specifically designed for sparse matrices and can handle missing values, while PCA works on dense matrices and requires complete data.

  2. Truncated SVD is often preferred for text data, while PCA is commonly used for numerical data.

  3. Truncated SVD is typically faster than PCA for large datasets, as it only computes a subset of the singular vectors and values.

How to does Truncated SVD identifies the most important features?

Truncated SVD does not directly identify the most frequent features in a dataset. Its primary goal is to reduce the dimensionality of the data while retaining the most important information. However, it is possible to indirectly identify the most frequent features by examining the singular vectors obtained from the truncated SVD.

In truncated SVD, the singular vectors are the linear combinations of the original features that explain the most variance in the data. Therefore, the features that have the highest coefficients in the singular vectors can be considered the most important features in the dataset.

Now let's do some coding...

To identify the most frequent features using truncated SVD, one could perform the following steps:

Step 1:

To keep it simple, I generate a random data matrix to simulate a dataset that I had in my real project.

from sklearn.decomposition import TruncatedSVD
import numpy as np # Generate a random data matrix X of size (m x n)
m, n = 1000, 1500
X = np.random.randn(m, n)

Step 2:

Now fit X to the truncated SVD to obtain the singular vectors. Then, compute the low-rank approximation.

k = 10 # number of singular vectors to keep
U, S, Vt = np.linalg.svd(X)
X_approx = U[:, :k] @ np.diag(S[:k]) @ Vt[:k, :] # fit data to the model

U: is the left singular value. It is a m*m matrix. The columns of this matrix are the corresponding eigenvectors of the singular values in the X matrix.

S: is the singular values matrix. It is a m*n matrix. The diagonal elements of S are the singular values of X.

Vt: is the right singular vectors matrix. It is n*n matrix. The columns of this matrix are the corresponding eigenvectors of the singular values in the X matrix.

Note that U captures the relationships among the columns of X, while Vt captures the relationships among the rows of X.

Step 3:

To evaluate the model, we have to calculate the relative approximation error like this:

approx_error = np.linalg.norm(X - X_approx) / np.linalg.norm(X)
print(f'Relative approximation error: {approx_error:.2f}')

Step 4:

Select the k most important features. For each singular vector, identify the features with the highest coefficients.

Step 5:

Compute the feature importance scores as the sum of absolute values of the coefficients in the first k singular vectors. Count the frequency of each feature across all singular vectors.

feature_importance = np.abs(Vk).sum(axis=0)

Step 6:

Sort the feature importance scores in descending order. Rank the features by frequency to identify the most frequent features in the dataset.

sorted_idx = np.argsort(feature_importance)[::-1] # Save the names of the top 10 most frequent features in a list
top_features = [feature_names[i] for i in sorted_idx[:10]]

Step 7:

Create a bar plot of the top 10 most frequent features:

plt.barh(range(10), feature_importance[sorted_idx[:10]])
plt.yticks(range(10), top_features)
plt.xlabel('Importance Score')
plt.title('Most Frequent Features in Truncated SVD')
plt.show()

Note that...

This approach assumes that the most frequent features are also the most important features in the dataset. This may not always be the case, as important features may have lower frequencies if they are correlated with other features that are more frequent.

Additionally, the choice of the truncation parameter in truncated SVD can affect the results, so it is important to choose an appropriate truncation level based on the problem at hand.

Kejkaew Thanasuan - Mar 22

item.title: 使用 ESLint 将代码带到美容院 | Take your code to the beauty salon with ESLint
item.description:
代码风格是软件开发过程中不可或缺的一部分。 也许团队发展中最困难的事情之一就是共同形成一种共同的风格。

为什么这很重要？

代码编写的统一使得维护和团队合作更容易。 开发人员可以更快地适应新模块或文件，因为他们已经熟悉既定的代码编写风格——非个人化代码可以减少认知负担。

当很难弄清楚是谁写的代码时，代码就会变得统一。

当同一件事可以通过多种方式完成时，linters 就会出手相助，并建立一个单一的“正确”方式。

为了让项目上的 ESLint 更严格和更统一的代码，我为 ESLint 编写了自己的插件 - Perfectionist 。

这个插件对导入、对象、TypeScript 接口和类型、JSX 道具和其他东西进行排序。

该插件有三种排序方式：字母排序、自然排序（类似于字母排序但更接近人类）和按字符串长度排序（上面的截图只是按行长度排序的示例）。

目前 ESLint Plugin Perfectionist 有 12 条规则，每条规则都可以自定义。

如何开始使用它？

首先，你需要在项目上安装 ESLint 本身：

 npm install --save-dev eslint

接下来，安装插件：

 npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-perfectionist

开始使用该插件的最简单方法是将三个现成的配置之一导入到您的.eslintrc配置中。 例如，按字母排序的配置：

 { "extends": [ "plugin:perfectionist/recommended-alphabetical" ] }

有关规则的其他配置和设置的详细信息，请参阅文档。

之后，linter 会在代码中显示很多错误。 但这不是问题，因为插件的所有规则都是可修复的。 这意味着安装后您不需要手动修复任何东西，只需运行带有标志--fix的 eslint，插件将自行修复所有内容。

让 linter 更严格，代码更漂亮！

感谢您的关注

github.com/azat-io/eslint-plugin-perfec… github.com/azat-io/eslint-plugin-perfec…



原文:

Code-style is an integral part of the software development process. And perhaps one of the most difficult things in team development is to come to a common style together.

Why is this important?

Uniformity in code writing makes it easier to maintain and teamwork. Developers can adapt more quickly to new modules or files because they are already familiar with the established style of writing code - impersonal code can reduce the cognitive load.

Code becomes uniform when it becomes difficult to figure out who wrote it.

And when the same thing can be done in several ways, linters come to the rescue and establish a single "right" way.

ESLint Plugin Perfectionist

In an effort to make ESLint stricter and more unified code on the project, I wrote my own plugin for ESLint - Perfectionist.

This plugin sorts imports, objects, TypeScript interfaces and types, JSX props and other stuff.

The plugin has three types of sorting: alphabetical sorting, natural sorting (similar to alphabetical sorting but closer to humans) and sorting by string length (the screenshot above is just an example of sorting by line length).

Currently ESLint Plugin Perfectionist has 12 rules and each of them can be customized.

How to start using it?

First of all, you need to install ESLint itself on the project:

npm install --save-dev eslint

Next, install the plugin:

npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-perfectionist

The easiest way to start using the plugin is to import one of the three ready-made configs into your .eslintrc config. For example, the config with alphabetical sorting:

{ "extends": [ "plugin:perfectionist/recommended-alphabetical" ]
}

See documentation for details on other configurations and settings for rules.

After that, the linter will show a lot of errors in the code. But this is not a problem, because all the rules of the plugin are fixable. This means that after installation you don't need to fix anything manually, just run eslint with flag --fix and plugin will fix everything by itself.

Let the linter be stricter and the code prettier!

Thank you for your attention

github.com/azat-io/eslint-plugin-perfec…
item.author: azat_io

item.title: 探索 | Evolution of Autosuggest at Bloomreach Bloomreach Autosuggest 的演变 | Discovery | Evolution of Autosuggest at Bloomreach
item.description:
发表于 6 月 8 日

电子商务平台通常难以理解客户的意图。 在 Bloomreach，我们使用多种工具、算法和系统来确定客户正在搜索什么，并且我们的平台不断学习如何提高意图实现的准确性和速度。 多年来，Autosuggest 系统已证明其作为一种工具的价值，不仅可以了解用户意图，还可以促进产品发现，以及为浏览网站的用户提供个性化指南。

每次迭代都使我们能够为我们的商家提供更多价值，并扩展系统以在互联网上一些最受欢迎的电子商务网站上为 Autosuggest 提供支持。

开端

多年来，一年一度的 Bloomreach 黑客马拉松已成为 Bloomreachers 演示从零开始到概念验证 (PoC) 的超过 48 小时开发的酷炫项目的试验台 最初的 Autosuggest 系统是使用Solr Suggester组件开发的，其中包含用户使用的建议数据从 Bloomreach 的数据分析系统生成的查询。 该系统很粗糙，但黑客马拉松演示非常棒，Bloomreachers 理所当然地认为该系统是游戏规则的改变者，尤其是因为它与我们最先进的排名引擎完美结合。

黑客马拉松的成功促使工程团队使用 PoC 作为跳板，不久我们就拥有了 Suggest v1.0，它更加完善，但使用了与黑客马拉松项目非常相似的方法。

缩放

我们推动了良好的用户体验，同时也对每次访问的收入产生了影响，这让商家非常满意。 多年来，该系统经过调整以允许使用黑名单建议、提升建议和调整排名算法等功能，同时还改进了整个表的性能。 然而，不久我们就开始遇到 Solr Suggester 的限制，这构成了我们系统的关键。 因此，是时候对系统进行一些重新设计了。 这让我们有机会从头开始审视 Autosuggest。 我们使用从商家那里收集的数据和反馈将新系统的要求分解为 3 个主要方面：质量、速度和可扩展性。

建议的质量：由于 Autosuggest 是用户与之交互的第一个界面，因此建议的质量可能是体验好坏的区别。 我们将优质建议定义为：

  1. 与商户目录相关。
  2. 用户输入的前缀的有效建议。
  3. 排名使得显示在列表较高位置的建议比显示在列表较低位置的建议更有可能被点击。

因此，拥有一个高性能的系统至关重要。 这意味着，即使使用 Bloomreach 的全球分布式数据中心，我们也需要高度关注性能数据，以确保为我们的用户提供最佳的 Autosuggest 体验。 现有系统速度很快（低于 50 毫秒 TP95 范围），但我们知道我们可以做得更好。

系统可扩展性：我们考虑了多项改进，以更好地识别和揭示客户意图。 使用初始系统，我们根本无法探索这些想法。 因此，我们希望自下而上地设计系统，以进一步探索改进 Autosuggest 的方法。

为了质量，我们使用了我们拥有的最佳用户意图来源——用户在商家网站上输入的实际查询。 事实证明，用户查询涵盖了很大一部分商家目录，因此质量和覆盖范围都非常好。 对这些运行一组噪声消除算法、频率分析和自动校正算法，我们可以得到一组既相关又可订购的查询，因为我们统计了用户搜索产品的频率，甚至搜索产品的频率。用户拼错了产品（我们看到的典型示例是将“gucci”拼错为“guci”）。

为了速度，我们决定将几乎所有的处理都推到后端，让前端简单地对预先生成的前缀映射执行查找。 生成的地图还考虑了拼写错误和更正，所以如果你搜索“guci”，你会得到与输入“gucci”相同的建议。 变得更聪明

现在我们有一个稳定的系统支持 Autosuggest，为我们的用户提供愉快的体验。 商家和我们的销售团队都非常赞赏 Autosuggest 的速度和意图识别！ 因此，这是进一步提高质量的最佳时机。 智能查询排名：到目前为止，用于排名建议的主要因素是搜索频率。 但是有了 A/B 测试框架，我们可以测试考虑信号的排名算法，包括但不限于 (a) 查询产生的收入，这取决于输入查询后购买的产品， (b) 查询的跳出率，即用户在搜索特定查询后没有购买任何东西的次数。

  • 通过结构化查询增加覆盖范围：Autosuggest 系统非常适合高流量商家，但对于低流量商家，我们根本没有足够的用户查询来创建覆盖整个商家目录的优质建议列表。 我们构建了一个我们称之为结构化查询的系统，它使用本体论和语法结合从产品中提取的产品属性来生成对产品和类别页面的查询。 假设我们有一个产品 P1 {color: “red”, product: “shoes”, brand: “nike”, style: “sporty”} 和一个包含 , <product> 和 <style> <brand> <product> 的本体. 我们将生成“red shoes”、“red nike sporty shoes”和“sporty nike shoes”作为 Autosuggest 系统的有效输入。</p></li> <li><p>更智能的前缀匹配：Autosuggest 的前缀匹配提出了它自己的挑战，这些挑战以多词建议为例。 当用户在搜索框中输入“iph”时，“apple iphone”将被认为是一个好的建议，但如果用户在同一个搜索框中输入“it”，那么“post it notes”将被认为是一个糟糕的建议。 在这两种情况下，搜索查询都是建议的第二个单词的前缀，但最终用户体验却大不相同。</p></li> </ul> <p>为了解决此类问题，我们构建了一个捕获前缀以及从列表中单击的建议的模型。 这个模型帮助我们理解前缀对于特定建议的相对重要性。 我们更新了我们的排名算法以使用此模型来合并建议的前缀重要性。 这意味着对于“iph”，“apple iphone”被提升了，而对于“it”，“post it notes”被推低了很多。 这允许系统在多词建议中执行更智能的前缀匹配。</p> <h2> <a name="up-up-and-away" href="#up-up-and-away" class= "anchor"> </a> Up, Up and Away </h2> <p>构建 Autosuggest 系统时，它旨在仅处理基于文本的建议。 然而，随着时间的推移，我们注意到 Autosuggest 系统还发挥了即时搜索的作用，将用户从主页直接带到他们感兴趣的产品页面。这种顿悟导致平台被扩展以提供即时搜索搜索解决方案，称为产品建议。 我们不想修改基本的 Autosuggest 响应，所以我们开发了一个 <a href="https://www.djangoproject.com/">基于 Django 的</a> 网络服务，它实现了 <a href="https ://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decorator_pattern">Decorator pattern</a> 在基本 Autosuggest 响应之上，允许通过来自外部系统的数据丰富建议。</p> <p>有了这个服务地方，开发产品建议很简单。 我们只使用第一个建议的原因是为了促进建议产品的相似性。 此外，系统相信顶部的建议最有可能被点击，因此与此建议相关的产品也更有可能被点击。</p> <p>在其核心，Autosuggest with Product Suggestions 意味着有 2 API 请求在幕后进行：一个是获取基本的 Autosuggest 响应，另一个是让产品与基本响应一起显示。 但是，这些 API 具有非常不同的延迟。 我们的 Autosuggest 系统的 TP95 延迟为 <; 如果我们为每个查询调用搜索 API 并等待响应，则不可能满足商家的建议 API 服务水平协议 (SLA) 要求。 同样，在调用每个建议 API 的同时进行搜索 API 调用会使搜索 API 系统过载，因为平均而言，建议 API 看到的搜索 API 请求数量大约是搜索 API 请求数量的 4 倍。</p> <p>在分析搜索调用时，我们观察到，来自 Autosuggest 系统的 Search API 发出的不同搜索查询的频率遵循长尾分布。 这一发现意味着大多数搜索 API 调用都是重复的，这使我们能够在 Autosuggest 系统中执行大量的搜索 API 响应缓存。 我们使用 Redis，它作为缓存系统经过了试用、测试和扩展。 当发生缓存未命中时，我们会按原样返回基本的 Autosuggest 响应，并对搜索 API 进行异步调用并将结果填充到我们的缓存中。 同一查询的下一个 API 请求在缓存中有产品，并且可以用产品建议装饰基本响应，而不会影响 API 延迟。 有了这个解决方案，98% 的建议请求都丰富了产品建议作为响应，同时延迟保持在 << 它与不久前在 BloomReach 黑客马拉松中 48 小时内编写的约 1000 行代码相去甚远。 该系统已经从最初的不起眼发展成为生产级主力，处理 <a href="https://www.bloomreach.com/en/blog/2018/04/system-performance-during-the -2017-holiday-season-at-bloomreach.html?_gl=1*1hiqgly*_ga*NDAwMzY1MjI3LjE2Nzg3MDY3NDA.*_ga_QNZ568TB7S*MTY4NjIyMjIzMC4yMS4xLjE2ODYyMjQwNjguNTguMC4w">1500 QPS 以上</a> 并保证 99.99% 的正常运行时间。 如今，我们商家网站上近 41% 的所有用户会话都是从点击 Autosuggest 建议开始的，这证明了所提供建议的质量。 每一次更改、每一次迭代、每一个架构决策都使系统一点一点地发展，以确保 Bloomreach 可以在目录大小、流量和收入大不相同的各种电子商务网站上为 Autosuggest 提供支持，而无需手动调整。 如果我要描述 Autosuggest 在 Bloomreach 中的成长故事，那么我认为我找不到比我们永远的 <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daft_Punk ">戴头盔的法国朋友</a>说得最好：“<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GDpmVUEjagg">更硬、更好、更快、更强</a>”。 </p> <p><strong><em>博客作者：Pratik Patre & 来自 Bloomreach 的 Naveen Pai，2018 年</em></strong></p>

    • 一旦暂停，Bloomreach 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

    解除暂停后，bloomreach 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，bloomreach 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 bloomreach 没有暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 bloomreach：

    降低 bloomreach 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    不屈不挠的 bloomreach 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Rory Warin for Bloomreach

    Posted on Jun 8

    eCommerce platforms often struggle to understand customer intent. At Bloomreach, we use multiple tools, algorithms and systems to figure out just what customers are searching for, and our platform is continuously learning how to improve accuracy and speed of intent fulfilment. Over the years, Autosuggest systems have proven their value as a tool to not just understand user intent, but to also promote discovery of products, as well as act as a personalized guide for the user browsing the website.

    The Autosuggest system at Bloomreach has gone through multiple iterations in terms of code, algorithms, and product scope. Each iteration has allowed us to provide more value to our merchants, and to scale the system to power Autosuggest on some of the most popular eCommerce websites on the internet.

    Inception

    The very first Autosuggest system at Bloomreach was developed at an internal hackathon. Over the years, the annual Bloomreach Hackathon has become a testbed for Bloomreachers to demo cool projects developed over 48 hours, from scratch to proof of concept (PoC) The initial Autosuggest system was developed using the Solr Suggester component with data for suggestions consumed from user generated queries from the Data Analytics systems at Bloomreach. The system was rough, but the hackathon demo was awesome, and Bloomreachers rightfully recognized the system as a game-changer, especially since it tied in perfectly with our state of the art ranking engine.

    Success at the hackathon led to the Engineering team using the PoC as a springboard and it wasn’t long before we had Suggest v1.0, which was more polished but used a very similar approach as the hackathon project.

    Scaling

    The Autosuggest system was very well received by our initial set of pilot merchants. We were driving a great user experience while also making an impact on the revenue per visit, and that led to very happy merchants. Over the years, the system was tweaked to allow features such as blacklisting suggestions, boosting suggestions, and tweaking ranking algorithms, while also making performance improvements across the table. However, it was not long before we started hitting the limitations of the Solr Suggester, which formed the crux of our system. So it was time for some re-engineering of the system. This gave us an opportunity to look at Autosuggest from the ground up. We used data and feedback we gathered from our merchants to break down requirements of the new system into 3 major buckets: quality, speed and extensibility.

    Quality of Suggestions: Since Autosuggest forms, the first interface that a user interacts with, the quality of suggestions can be the difference between a good and an awful experience. We define good quality suggestions as those which are:

    1. Relevant to the merchant catalogue.
    2. Valid suggestions for the prefix typed by the user.
    3. Ranked such that the suggestions shown higher up the list are more likely to be clicked on than the ones shown lower on the list.

    The speed of Autosuggest: The human mind finds a lag greater than 150ms between typing a query and a change in Autosuggest very jarring. As such, it is of critical importance to have a highly performant system. This means that, even with Bloomreach’s globally distributed data centers, we need to keep a strong focus on performance numbers to ensure the best Autosuggest experience for our users. The existing system was fast (in the sub-50ms TP95 range) but we knew we could do much better.

    System Extensibility: There were multiple improvements we had in mind to better identify and surface customer intent. With the initial system, we simply could not explore these ideas. So from the bottom up, we wanted to design the system to allow further exploration of ways to improve Autosuggest.

    For quality, we went to the best source of user intent we have - actual queries typed in by users on the merchant website. As it turned out, user queries cover a massive percentage of the merchant catalogue, so the quality and coverage are both really good. Running a set of noise removal algorithms,frequency analysis and auto-correction algorithms on these gives us a set of queries which are both relevant as well as orderable, since we have statistics on how often a user searches for a product or even how often a user misspells a product (The quintessential example we saw was ‘gucci’ misspelled as ‘guci’).

    For speed, we decided to push almost all the processing to the backend, leaving the frontend to simply perform a lookup on a pre-generated prefix map. The generated map also took into account misspellings and corrections, so if you searched for “guci” you would be shown the same suggestions as if you had typed “gucci”. Solr continued to power our serving infrastructure, happily performing the prefix matching for user queries and lookup.

    Getting Smarter

    Now we had a stable system powering Autosuggest, delivering a delightful experience to our users. The speed and intent identification of Autosuggest was much appreciated, both by merchants and our sales teams! ;)

    So it was the perfect time to improve quality even further. The Autosuggest team decided to focus on the following focus areas:

    • A/B Testing: There is no better way to prove that system A is better than B than by having data to back the claim. A/B testing goes a long way to gather data which can help test theories. The Autosuggest system was extended to support A/B testing to allow different Autosuggest experiences to be provided to a user based on cookies.

    • Smart Query Ranking: So far, the major factor used for ranking suggestions was the frequency with which it was searched. But with an A/B testing framework in place, we could test out ranking algorithms that took into account signals including, but not limited to, (a) revenue generated by a query, which depends on products purchased once the query is typed in, and (b) bounce rate of a query, which is the number of times a user didn’t buy anything after searching for a particular query.

    • Increasing Coverage with Structured Queries: The Autosuggest system worked great for high traffic merchants, but for low traffic merchants, we simply did not have enough user queries to create a list of quality suggestions which would cover the entire merchant catalogue. We built a system we call Structured Queries, which generates queries for product and category pages using ontologies and grammars combined with product attributes extracted from the product. Suppose we had a product P1 with {color: “red”, product: “shoes”, brand: “nike”, style: “sporty”} and an ontology containing , <product> and <style> <brand> <product>. We would generate “red shoes”, “red nike sporty shoes” and “sporty nike shoes” as valid inputs to the Autosuggest system.</p></li> <li><p>Smarter Prefix Matching: Prefix matching for Autosuggest presents it’s own challenges, which get exemplified with multi-word suggestions. When a user types in “iph” in a search box, “apple iphone” would be considered a good suggestion, but if a user types “it” in the same search box then “post it notes” is a bad suggestion. In both these cases the search query is a prefix of the second word of the suggestion, but the end user experience is vastly different.</p></li> </ul> <p>To solve such problems, we built a model which captures the prefix along with the suggestion clicked from the list. This model helps us understand the relative importance of a prefix for a particular suggestion. We updated our ranking algorithm to use this model to incorporate the prefix-importance for a suggestion. This meant that for “iph”, “apple iphone” was boosted up, while for “it”,”post it notes” was pushed much lower. This allowed the system to perform smarter prefix matching in a multi-word suggestions.</p> <h2> <a name="up-up-and-away" href="#up-up-and-away" class="anchor"> </a> Up, Up and Away </h2> <p>When the Autosuggest system was built, it was meant to handle only text-based suggestions. However, over time, we noticed that Autosuggest systems also play the role of an instant-search by taking a user from the home page directly to a product page they are interested in. This epiphany lead to the platform being extended to provide an instant-search solution, known as Product Suggestions. We didn’t want to modify the base Autosuggest response so we developed a <a href="https://www.djangoproject.com/">Django-based</a> web service which implemented the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decorator_pattern">Decorator pattern</a> on top of the base Autosuggest response, allowing suggestions to be enriched by data from external systems.</p> <p>With this service in place, developing Product Suggestions was straightforward. The Autosuggest system could easily be extended to use the existing Bloomreach search infrastructure to generate a list of products which are the top search results for the first suggestion for the base Autosuggest response. The reason we used only the first suggestion was to promote similarity in suggested products. Also, the system trusted that the suggestion at the top was most likely to be clicked so products relevant to this suggestion are also more likely to be clicked.</p> <p>At its core, Autosuggest with Product Suggestions meant there were 2 API requests being made behind the scenes: one to get the base Autosuggest response, and the other to get the products to show alongside the base response. However, these APIs have very different latencies. Our Autosuggest system had a TP95 latency of < 30ms while our search API request had a TP95 latency of ~300ms. It would be impossible to meet Suggest API Service Level Agreement (SLA) requirements for merchants if we made a search API call for every query and waited for the response. Similarly, making a search API call alongside every suggest API call would overload the search API system since, on average, the suggest API sees about 4x the number of search API requests.</p> <p>When the search calls were analyzed, we observed that the frequency of different search queries made by the Search API from the Autosuggest system follows a long tail distribution. This discovery implied that most search API calls are repetitive, allowing us to perform massive caching of search API responses within the Autosuggest system. We use Redis, which is tried, tested and scales beautifully as a caching system. When a cache miss happens, we return the base Autosuggest response as is, and make an asynchronous call to the Search API and populate the results in our cache. The next API request for the same query has products in the cache and can decorate the base response with product suggestions without having the API latency take a hit. With this solution, 98% of suggest requests are enriched with product suggestions in response while latencies remain well within the < 30ms range.</p> <h2> <a name="harder-better-faster-stronger" href="#harder-better-faster-stronger" class="anchor"> </a> Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger </h2> <p>A massive amount of product design and engineering development has gone into making Bloomreach AutoSuggest the system it is today. It has come very far from the ~1000 lines of code written over 48 hours in the BloomReach hackathon not so long ago. The system has grown from its humble beginnings to a production grade workhorse, handling an Autosuggest API load of <a href="https://www.bloomreach.com/en/blog/2018/04/system-performance-during-the-2017-holiday-season-at-bloomreach.html?_gl=1*1hiqgly*_ga*NDAwMzY1MjI3LjE2Nzg3MDY3NDA.*_ga_QNZ568TB7S*MTY4NjIyMjIzMC4yMS4xLjE2ODYyMjQwNjguNTguMC4w">upwards of 1500 QPS</a> and an assured uptime of 99.99%. Today, nearly 41% of all user sessions on our merchant websites begin with a clicked Autosuggest suggestion, which is a testament to the quality of suggestions served. Every change, every iteration, every architectural decision has grown the system bit by bit to ensure Bloomreach can power Autosuggest on a vast variety of eCommerce sites, with vastly differing catalogue sizes, traffic, and revenue, with no manual tweaking required. The next frontier for the Autosuggest product is to serve personalized suggestions and to improve suggestions based on user context. If I were to describe the story of the growth of Autosuggest within Bloomreach, then I don’t think I could find better words than those spoken by our eternally <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daft_Punk">helmeted French friends</a> who said it best: “<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GDpmVUEjagg">Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger</a>”.</p> <p><strong><em>Blog written by: Pratik Patre & Naveen Pai from Bloomreach, 2018</em></strong></p>

    John Mark Bulabos - Jun 8

    John Mark Bulabos - Jun 8

    Eder Munhoz dos Santos - Jun 8

    Once suspended, bloomreach will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, bloomreach will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by bloomreach will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If bloomreach is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag bloomreach:

    Make all posts by bloomreach less visible

    bloomreach consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging bloomreach will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: rorywarin

item.title: 最新帖子 | New post
item.description:
一旦暂停，hussain_joe 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，hussain_joe 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，hussain_joe 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 hussain_joe 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 hussain_joe 的方法：

降低 hussain_joe 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 hussain_joe 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, hussain_joe will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, hussain_joe will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by hussain_joe will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If hussain_joe is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag hussain_joe:

Make all posts by hussain_joe less visible

hussain_joe consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging hussain_joe will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 学习欧洲葡萄牙语 | Learn European Portuguese
item.description:
学习一门全新的语言既令人兴奋又充满乐趣。 当您学习葡萄牙语时，您将能够与认识该语言的人交谈，无论您是在阿尔加维度假还是在为愉快的葡萄牙语杂货店聊天。

您可以选择通过互联网学习这门语言，也可以购买一台基于完全指导的电脑来学习这门语言。 另一个你不得不面对的选择是一所大学，他们提供语言出版物和葡萄牙语。
学习欧洲葡萄牙语
确保您能够注意所说的语言。 这不会最有效地美化您的单词发音，但也可以帮助您更快地学习语言。 与朋友或伴侣一起报名参加培训会对您有所帮助，并且您可以一起锻炼。

如果您选择通过在线指导或基于笔记本电脑的路线观看，最好找到一个带有音频选项的。 通过这种方式，您可以聆听他们谈论该语言并上传您的语言知识。

在学习一门新语言的同时，练习普通语言至关重要。 你可以通过自己或与他人一起做到这一点，即使他们不会说这种语言。 在您学习新事物时，简单地练习单词会有所帮助。

你选择的方向形式甚至会影响你说这门语言的方式。 选择一条高度例外的路径，这样您就可以从中获得额外的优势并更快地学习语言。 这些通常还会使路线更加令人兴奋，并让您学习得更快。

开始学习一门新语言需要学习该语言的基础知识。 对于葡萄牙语，它没有任何特色。 您将开始掌握主要的短语、发音、字母和数字，以便您能够流利地使用该语言。

一旦基础知识得到保护，您就可以继续学习该语言更复杂的部分。 这将包括形成句子并将规则应用于语言的口头和书面形式。 这很关键，可以增强您的语言专业知识。

学习一门全新的语言后，您可以做很多事情。 无论您是需要用葡萄牙语说、写还是看电影，一旦您研究了这门语言，您就可以做到。 了解两种语言总是一个令人惊奇的想法，而葡萄牙语使它变得有趣和令人兴奋。

用他们自己的语言与他人交谈会给您带来令人敬畏的成就感。 一旦您会说自己的语言，您就不必再次站着等待翻译。 将这种语言添加到您的绘画能力中也是极好的。



原文:

Learning a brand new language is thrilling and fun. When you analyze Portuguese you will be in a position to talk to individuals who recognize the language whether you're on a holiday in Algarve or clearly chatting for your pleasant Portuguese grocer.

You can pick out to examine this language over the internet or you can purchase a pc based totally direction with the intention to educate you the language. Another choice you've got to be had to you is a college wherein they offer language publications along with Portuguese.
Learn European Portuguese
Make positive which you are in a position to pay attention the language spoken. This will not most effective beautify your pronunciation of the words however additionally help you learn the language faster. Signing up for a direction with a pal or a partner will help you and you may exercise collectively.

Should you choose to look at via an online direction or a laptop primarily based route, it is a superb idea to locate one with an audio alternative. This way you may listen them talk the language and upload on your knowledge of the language.

Practicing ordinary is vital whilst learning a new language. You can do this by way of your self or with someone else, even supposing they do now not talk the language. Simply practising the words help whilst you are studying something new.

The form of direction you pick will even have an effect on the manner you talk the language. Choose a path that is of a high exceptional so you can gain extra advantages from it and examine the language faster. These additionally generally tend to make the route extra exciting and have you studying a lot faster.

Beginning a new language will involve studying about the basics of that language. With Portuguese it's miles no distinctive. You will start mastering the primary phrases, pronunciations, letters and numbers for your adventure to becoming fluent within the language.

Once the fundamentals have been protected, you may pass on to the greater complex parts of the language. This will encompass forming sentences and applying rules to the spoken and written form of the language. This is critical and could enhance your expertise of the language.

There is a lot you may do after you learn a brand new language. Whether you need to speak, write or watch movies in Portuguese, you may be able to do so once you research the language. It is constantly an amazing concept to realize a couple of language and Portuguese makes it amusing and exciting.

Speaking to others of their own tongue will provide you with a awesome feeling of accomplishment. You by no means need to stand round looking ahead to a translator once more as soon as you could speak the language your self. It is also excellent to add this language for your paintings competencies.
